title: Quiz: Kernel Alignment and Optimization
description: Test your understanding of kernel alignment concepts

questions.question: What does kernel-target alignment (KTA) measure?

options.text: The computational cost of the kernel
How well the kernel separates classes defined by the labels
The dimensionality of the feature space

questions.explanation: Kernel-target alignment measures how well a kernel matrix K aligns with the ideal target kernel yy^T, indicating how well the kernel separates the classes defined by labels y.

questions.question: What is the formula for kernel alignment between K₁ and K₂?
questions.explanation: Kernel alignment is the normalized Frobenius inner product: A(K₁, K₂) = ⟨K₁, K₂⟩_F / (||K₁||_F ||K₂||_F), giving a value between -1 and 1.

questions.question: What is the range of kernel alignment values?
questions.explanation: Kernel alignment is a normalized measure bounded between -1 and 1, similar to a correlation coefficient. A value of 1 indicates perfect alignment.

questions.question: Centering the kernel matrix before computing KTA helps handle class imbalance.
questions.explanation: True. Centering the kernel matrix (K_c = HKH where H is the centering matrix) removes the mean from the feature space, making KTA more robust to class imbalance.

questions.question: When optimizing quantum kernel parameters using KTA, what should we do?
questions.explanation: We maximize KTA during kernel optimization because higher KTA indicates better alignment with the classification target, meaning the kernel better separates the classes.

questions.question: What is the ideal target kernel K_target for binary classification with labels y ∈ {-1, +1}?
questions.explanation: The ideal target kernel is K_target = yy^T, where K_target[i,j] = y_i * y_j. This gives +1 for same-class pairs and -1 for different-class pairs.

questions.question: Which metric measures the ratio of within-class to between-class kernel similarity?
questions.explanation: Class separability measures the ratio of within-class similarity to between-class similarity. Higher values indicate that the kernel assigns higher similarity to same-class pairs.

questions.question: Which are benefits of using kernel alignment for quantum kernel optimization? (Select all)

options.text: Provides a differentiable objective for gradient-based training
Doesn't require training an SVM to evaluate kernel quality
Guarantees finding the globally optimal kernel
Can be computed efficiently from the kernel matrix

questions.explanation: KTA is differentiable (enabling gradient-based optimization), can be computed directly from the kernel matrix without SVM training, and is computationally efficient. However, it doesn't guarantee global optimality.
